title: internal_dynamics
description: Per-candidate PCA-based preliminary class assignment (HOM_REF / HET / HOM_INV) from STEP_C01i_decompose.R, with per-window class consistency, silhouette + BIC quality metrics, flashlight seeding status, and per-window class track (stored separately as an RDS file named by per_window_class_file). This block is chat-12's rewrite scope: the FINAL class assignment (including RECOMBINANT / RECOMBINANT_GC / RECOMBINANT_DCO) is produced by STEP_C01i_d_seal and written to the sample_registry groups table; this block holds only the PCA preliminary. The per-candidate recombinant map lives in the recombinant_map block, written by STEP_C01i_b_multi_recomb (pre-chat-12 the two were conflated in a single internal_dynamics block; that older flavor is archived at _archive_superseded/bk_schemas_pre_canonical/internal_dynamics.schema.json.pre_BK). Three emit variants: (a) full success with silhouette / BIC / per_sample; (b) no_seeding stub when seed_loader returns no seeds (decomp_status='no_seeding' + decomp_reason); (c) no_seeding stub when seeded_kmeans falls back to unsupervised (per chat-9 design we do NOT ship unsupervised). Downstream scripts must read decomp_status before dereferencing rich fields. See BK_KEYS_EXPLAINED.md in this directory for per-key documentation (biological meaning, provenance, downstream consumer).
question: Q1, Q2
group_validation_required: NONE (this block produces the initial grouping that seal then synthesises)

decomp_status.description: Chat-12: explicit status so downstream readers can distinguish 'succeeded with seeds' from 'skipped because seeds were insufficient'.

decomp_reason.description: Only populated when decomp_status == 'no_seeding'. Names the specific reason (seed_loader verdict, or 'seeded_kmeans fell back to unsupervised — not shipping').

seed_source.description: Which seed loader branch produced the seeds: e.g. 'flashlight+step03', 'step03', 'flashlight', 'flashlight_legacy'.

n_seed_agree.description: Number of samples where multiple seed sources agree.

n_seed_conflict.description: Number of samples dropped because seed sources conflicted (Finding AY: implementation is drop-on-conflict, stricter than chat-9 design language of 'priority-pick').

decomp_quality.description: Chat-13 AV non-gating annotation derived from silhouette; 'clean' if silhouette >= 0.40 else 'noisy'. C01d / C01f must NOT promote on this field during the first wiring pass.

bic_gap_k3_vs_k2.description: >0 means k=3 fits better than k=2.

phase_concordance.description: Fraction of HET samples with phased Clair3 support.

sv_prior_mode.description: Which seed-loader branch produced the seeds: e.g. 'sv_prior+step03', 'step03', 'sv_prior', 'sv_prior_legacy'. Renamed 2026-04-24 from 'flashlight_mode' to match decompose's writer vocabulary.

n_discordant.description: Samples where the SV anchor disagrees with the clustering call.

n_cheat2_reclassified.description: Samples moved HOM_INV -> HET by the het-DEL at breakpoint constraint (cheat2).

per_sample.description: One entry per sample in the candidate (only in decomp_status='seeded_ok' blocks).

window_consistency.description: Fraction of windows whose local class matches the sample's global class.

per_window_class_file.description: RDS filename (alongside the block JSON) carrying the per-window x per-sample class matrix. Too big for JSON. Example: 'per_window_class.rds'.

ancestry_div_hom_ref_vs_hom_inv.description: L1 distance between mean Q vectors of the HOM_REF and HOM_INV classes at this candidate locus. Folded in from the superseded band_composition schema (2026-04-24). Computed by joining decompose's per-sample class assignment with Engine B's per-window local-Q vectors inside the candidate interval, averaging Q per class, then taking sum(|q_ref - q_inv|) across K. High values indicate the two homozygous classes come from different ancestral subpopulations (family-driven signal, not a genuine inversion). Null until the decompose writer is extended to pull instant_q Q; the key is declared here now so downstream readers can code against the canonical location.

keys_extracted._comment_band_composition_fold: Folded in from the superseded band_composition.schema.json (archived 2026-04-24). The standalone schema had only 2 keys and shared this block's writer; promoting them here closes the PRODUCES_BUT_NOT_WIRED gap. Names carry a 'decompose_' prefix so readers can see at a glance that the fact is sourced from STEP_C01i_decompose.R — distinguishes from q2_pca_k_used (same underlying field, different q-axis interpretation) and from any future ancestry-divergence computed by a different script.
